task_completion.description: Verify the creation of a dashboard named 'Splunk_Usage_Audit'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Splunk Dashboards list shows 'Splunk_Usage_Audit', or the dashboard is open with 'Splunk_Usage_Audit' as the title.

task_completion.description: Verify the dashboard contains at least three panels visualizing user search activity.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Splunk_Usage_Audit' dashboard displays 3 or more distinct visualization panels (e.g., charts, tables, or single values) related to search activity.

task_completion.description: Verify the dashboard panels filter out the internal system user.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The search queries for the dashboard panels (visible in edit mode, search bar, or SPL view) explicitly exclude the system user, e.g., using `user!="splunk-system-user"` or `NOT user="splunk-system-user"`.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of a scheduled alert named 'Data_Exfiltration_Via_Search'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Splunk Alerts list shows 'Data_Exfiltration_Via_Search', or the alert configuration/save dialog is visible with this exact name.

task_completion.description: Verify the alert monitors the `_audit` index for the specified data export commands.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The alert's underlying search query includes `index=_audit` and the keywords `outputcsv`, `outputlookup`, and `export`.

task_completion.description: Verify the alert is configured to run on an hourly schedule.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The alert configuration screen shows the schedule type set to run hourly (e.g., 'Run every hour' selected in the UI, or a cron expression like `0 * * * *`).

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Splunk web interface to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Splunk Web UI (http://localhost:8000) being navigated in a web browser, rather than API calls via terminal.

integrity.description: The agent used the Splunk UI to create the dashboard and alert, rather than manually writing configuration files.
integrity.visual_evidence: Absence of terminal windows or text editors (like nano/vim) editing `.conf` or `.xml` files directly in the Splunk backend directories.

integrity.description: The agent actively used the Search & Reporting app to build the queries before saving them.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Splunk Search interface is visible with search results populating the screen before being saved as a dashboard panel or alert.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Dashboard must be named exactly 'Splunk_Usage_Audit' and contain a minimum of 3 panels. The dashboard searches must explicitly filter out 'splunk-system-user'. The alert must be named exactly 'Data_Exfiltration_Via_Search', target the '_audit' index, and look for the keywords 'outputcsv', 'outputlookup', and 'export'. The alert must be scheduled to run hourly.
